Now I see:
- The dark gray frame (node 9, color G) is gone
- The blue center (node 9, color b) is still there at [[37, 33], [37, 34], [38, 34], [38, 33]]
- The white block (node 8) expanded from [[36, 31], [39, 31]] to [[36, 31], [36, 35], [39, 35], [39, 31]] - it now fills the space where the frame was!
- Node 10 is now color P (pink) at [[63, 0], [63, 60]] - this is the HUD bar
So SPACE removed the dark gray frame from the blue box, and the white block expanded to fill that space. The blue center remains.
The goal might be to collect all three blue boxes by removing their frames. Now I need to:
1. Navigate to the left blue box (cols 16-19, rows 28-31)
2. Navigate to the right blue box (cols 44-47, rows 24-27)
3. Use SPACE on each to remove their frames
But wait - now the player is blocked by the expanded white block. Let me check if I can move. The white block is now at [[36, 31], [36, 35], [39, 35], [39, 31]], which is rows 36-39, cols 31-35. The player is at rows 36-39, cols 28-30. So the white block is to the right of the player.
Let me try moving UP to get above the white block and navigate to the other blue boxes.

action(['UP'])
